title: Pinterest Just Got a Wall Street Pile-On: Six Firms Hike Price Targets After Q1 Beat Crushes Estimates
text: Pinterest received strong analyst support following its Q1 2026 earnings beat. At least seven sell-side firms raised their price targets, with PINS stock trading up 11% intraday to $23 following the earnings announcement. Goldman Sachs led the bull camp, citing Pinterest's resilience in lower-funnel advertising, healthy user engagement, SMB and mid-market strength, AI-driven ad automation, search monetization, EBITDA upside, and an increased pace of share repurchases. The consensus upgraded rating reflected optimism about Pinterest's growth trajectory driven by AI shopping innovations and strategic acquisitions like tvScientific. Analyst coverage showed strong confidence in the company's ability to drive revenue and earnings growth going forward.
